jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**About the Mission**  
The Executive Director is the champion of The Avenue’s unique community impact. We are looking for a visionary leader with a deep\-seated passion for:

* **Distinctive Programming:** Delivering a one\-of\-a\-kind music format to our community 24/7\.
* **Community Support:** Providing local nonprofits with unprecedented airwave access for the greater good.
* **Artist Advocacy:** Offering a vital platform for local and regional artists to share their music.

**The Role**

As Executive Director, you will hold primary responsibility for the strategic direction, financial health, and cultural vitality of the station. You will lead a dedicated team, engage with our Board of Directors (BOD), and serve as the primary "storyteller" to our supporters and partners.

**Core Responsibilities**

* **Strategic Leadership:** Set the overall direction for the station and manage the performance and well\-being of the staff.
* **Revenue \& Fundraising:** Formulate and execute comprehensive fundraising strategies. You will drive growth across donor development, sponsorships, and special events.
* **Financial Stewardship:** Collaborate with the BOD and staff to create annual budgets, ensuring strict adherence and long\-term financial sustainability.
* **Relationship Management:** Act as the face of The Avenue. You will meet with donors, clients, and vendors to articulate our unique benefits and secure vital partnerships.
* **Team Development:** Support and manage direct reports, fostering a culture of results and local outreach.
* **Growth Innovation:** Work closely with the team to identify and seize new opportunities for expansion and community engagement.

**Key Attributes \& Qualifications**

We are seeking a results\-oriented leader who can inspire internal staff and external stakeholders alike.

* **Proven Leadership:** Extensive experience in organizational management and team leadership.
* **Financial Acumen:** Strong background in financial management and accountability.
* **Fundraising Expertise:** A track record of success in local fundraising or sales, ideally with existing contacts in the market. Experience in the nonprofit sector is a significant plus.
* **Strategic Mindset:** The ability to plan for long\-term station growth while simultaneously overseeing daily fundraising initiatives.
* **Communication Skills:** Expert\-level ability to tell The Avenue’s story in a way that engages donors and partners.
* **Mission Alignment:** A genuine appreciation for The Avenue’s format and community\-centric goals.

*The Avenue is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.*

Pay: $75,000\.00 \- $95,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Appleton, WI 54911

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
**EXODUS MINISTRIES** is a Dallas\-based Christian nonprofit organization that reunites formerly incarcerated single mothers with their children and provides an array of services necessary for these women to become responsible, productive, and self\-sufficient. We provide a safe and secure apartment residence (privately owned) for these families to live in, and the tools and support needed for long\-term success. Each of our women journeys through a 12\-month residential program of treatment and support services and is fully embraced by a broad community of volunteers; mentors; classroom instructors; trained counselors; and thoughtful programming for school\-age kids to guide these families on a path to real change.

**DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS**

**GENERAL PURPOSE** of this position is to manage and strengthen the operational infrastructure of Exodus Ministries. Reporting directly to the Executive Director, this position oversees the physical property, ensures operational systems and policies run efficiently, manages vendor relationships, and leads initiatives that promote the long\-term success and safety of the apartment complex and its programs.

The Director of Operations is responsible for ensuring and improving the performance, productivity, and efficiency of facility operations through the support and maintenance of established methods and strategies. This position will safeguard and expand property efficiencies to secure long\-term program success among Exodus’ targeted constituents.

**Main Job Tasks, Duties and Responsibilities**

* **Facilities \& Property Management** \- Lead the planning, coordination, and execution of facility\-related projects and ongoing property maintenance. This includes (but is not limited to) all vehicle maintenance and building upkeep. Additionally, this role will maintain strong working relationships with all vendors and service providers involved in facilities and operational support.
* **Organizational Operations** \- Support the smooth daily functioning of the Exodus property by developing and implementing systems that promote efficiency and effectiveness. This role will monitor adherence to program rules and protocols, including resident apartment inspections and other program compliance measures.
* **Project Planning \& Resource Management** – Manage inventory and procurement of office and program supplies. Provide logistical support across departments to facilitate events, trainings, and organizational activities. Assist with scheduling and coordination for facility use, volunteer engagement, and special projects.
* **Strategic \& Administrative Support** \- Collaborate with the Executive Director on overall property efficiency. Track and report on performance metrics related to facilities and operations. Identify and implement improvements to operational systems and physical infrastructure.

**EDUCATION \& EXPERIENCE**

The formal education and experience for the Director of Operations job description varies according to the nature of the job responsibilities. However, the following are commonly required in the Director of Operations role:

* Prefer bachelors degree in business administration, nonprofit management, counseling, facilities management, or a related field.
* Five (5\) years of experience in operations, facilities, or project management—preferably in a nonprofit or community\-based organization.
* Strong operational track record is needed.
* Background in social services or counseling is a plus.

**KEY SKILLS \& COMPETENCIES**

* Should be capable of strong ethical leadership; collaborate with the Executive Director to build an environment of collective responsibility and accountability.
* Must have strong writing and oral communication skills.
* Outstanding communication and people skills.
* Knowledge and experience in organizational effectiveness and operations management.
* Knowledge of business and management principles and practices.
* Knowledge of project management principles and practices.
* Information technology skills; Microsoft tools proficiency.
* A heart for the Exodus mission and a commitment to empowering women and families.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $49,000\.00 \- $52,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Employee assistance program
* Paid time off

Ability to Commute:

* Dallas, TX 75204 (Required)

Ability to Relocate:

* Dallas, TX 75204: Relocate before starting work (Required)

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Deputy Director
jobs.description:
**Deputy Director** **\-** **(****26141848****)**  

Few positions in Montana state government touch more of what makes Montana, Montana. The Deputy Director of The Montana Department of Natural Resources and Conservation helps lead the agency responsible for the state’s land, water, forests, and wildland fire protection — 5\.2 million surface acres of state trust land, water rights administration statewide, and a network of 35 field offices serving communities in every corner of the state. The work carries substantial economic impact and generational consequence: DNRC’s mission is to help ensure Montana’s land and water resources provide benefits for present and future generations.


We are seeking a proven executive to serve as the Department’s second\-in\-command — a leader of leaders who can run day\-to\-day operations of a complex, geographically distributed organization while advising the Director on the issues that shape Montana’s natural resource future.

**The Role:**


The Deputy Director is the principal advisor to the Director and has primary responsibility for the Department’s day\-to\-day operations. The Deputy assists with planning, administration, personnel management, budget development, communications, and establishing goals and direction for the Department; serves as Acting Director in the Director’s absence; and acts on behalf of the Director at internal and external meetings, before the Legislature, and with federal, tribal, and local partners.


A typical day may involve solving complex natural resource management issues with substantial economic impacts; resolving constituent conflicts or complaints; state personnel policy and pay decisions; long\-range program and fiscal strategy; internal and external communication; and management of substantial real estate, physical infrastructure, and agency liability. The Deputy regularly resolves competing demands between divisions, allocates agency resources, interprets and enforces agency policy, and responds to the Governor’s Office, constituent groups, state legislators, Montana’s congressional delegation, and state, federal, and tribal partners.

**What You’ll Walk Into:**
==========================


This is a consequential moment to join DNRC’s executive team. Among the priorities the next Deputy Director will help lead:

* **The upcoming legislative session:** the Deputy coordinates the Department’s biennial budget preparation and legislative proposals with the Governor’s Office and division administrators and represents the Department at hearings and committee meetings.
* **Statewide operations at scale:** the Deputy supports five divisions and 35 field offices spanning trust land management, forestry and fire protection, water resources, conservation, and oil and gas conservation.
* **Coordinating and implementing organizational efficiencies:** the Deputy identifies operational efficiencies, including opportunities in fiscal management to create transparency and sound budget management practices for an annual budget of more than $100 million.

**Who You’ll Lead:**
====================


The Deputy Director reports to the Director and directly supervises the Chief Financial Officer, Communications Director, Safety Manager, Geographic Information System (GIS) Manager, two Administrative Law Judges, and a Legal Secretary, and indirectly supervises the agency's Human Resources Officer, providing oversight of administrative, technical, and professional work performed across the Department.

**What We’re Looking For:**
===========================

* Successful candidates will be able to demonstrate:
* A track record of leading operations across a large, complex, or geographically distributed organization, including supervising senior leaders.
* Experience presenting budgets, legislation, or policy proposals to elected bodies, governing boards, or oversight agencies, as well as navigating the legislative process.
* A history of successfully managing high\-stakes issues to resolution with a high degree of integrity, including issues where stakeholders hold polarized views of what constitutes reasonable use of land and water.
* The ability to build and sustain effective working relationships with staff at every level and with external partners such as elected officials, county commissioners, and tribal leadership.
* Advanced skill in planning, prioritizing, and goal setting, and high proficiency in organizational awareness, conflict management, negotiation, and energizing others.
* Demonstrated excellence in oral and written communication.

 **APPLICATION MATERIALS REQUIRED FOR THIS POSITION ARE:**

* **Cover Letter**\- In your cover letter, please describe your experience leading operations in a complex organization and your experience with legislative or budget processes.
* **Resume**
 **TIP: When attaching your resume, cover letter, and other material, you must mark the attachments as "relevant".**

  

The selection process includes, at minimum, an in\-person panel interview and a second interview with the Director; finalists may be asked to complete a brief work\-sample exercise. **Benefits:**  

Live and work in Helena, Montana, which offers a unique blend of opportunities and natural beauty, situated between Yellowstone and Glacier National Parks. Montana offers abundant recreational opportunities, including camping, fishing, hunting, mountain biking, and hiking.  

Additional benefits include:* Work/life balance.
* Generous paid leave (vacation, sick, holidays), with vacation leave increasing with service.
* Longevity pay with continued state service.
* Health, dental, vision, life insurance, and flexible spending accounts.
* Access to free employee medical clinics and telehealth.
* Retirement plans (Defined Benefit and Defined Contribution plus an optional 457(b) Plan).
* Public Service Loan Forgiveness \- may qualify you for student loan forgiveness.
* Career progression, professional development, and state employe

jobs.title: Executive Director - ProAmpac Employee Assistance Fund (PEAF)
jobs.description:
**About the Role**
------------------



As the **Executive Director**, you will oversee all strategic, financial, and operational aspects of the ProAmpac Employee Assistance Fund (PEAF). You will carry out the vision of the organization, leading efforts to scale the fund globally, engage our workforce, and ensure the fund's long\-term sustainability.



Whether you are navigating compliance, collaborating with the Board of Directors, or visiting our manufacturing sites to raise awareness, your leadership will directly support employees when they need it most.

  


**Essential Duties and Responsibilities**
-----------------------------------------


### **1\. Strategic Leadership \& Global Expansion**


* **Drive Vision:** Provide overall leadership and direction for the PEAF, aligning the program with ProAmpac's core values: Integrity, Intensity, Innovation, Involvement, and Impact.
* **Expand Our Reach:** Lead the expansion of the fund to international locations and newly acquired manufacturing sites, adapting processes to respect regional HR policies and practices.
* **Growth Strategy:** Develop and execute strategic plans to scale program participation, overall funding, and community impact.

### **2\. Program Administration \& Compliance**


* **Daily Operations:** Oversee the day\-to\-day administration of the grant process, ensuring applications are handled fairly, efficiently, and with the **strictest confidentiality**.
* **Regulatory Compliance:** Maintain rigorous records of all grants, donor contributions, and fund activities. Ensure full compliance with nonprofit governance, tax regulations, and legal guidelines.
* **Digital Resources:** Manage the PEAF website and promotional materials to ensure easy, seamless access to applications and contribution portals.

### **3\. Board Governance \& Financial Sustainability**


* **Board Collaboration:** Partner closely with the Board of Directors to guide policy development, long\-term planning, and strategic decisions.
* **Reporting:** Prepare and deliver program metrics, financial updates, and impact reports for board meetings.
* **Fundraising:** Design and champion creative fundraising initiatives, including annual donor campaigns, employee payroll deduction programs, and special events.

### **4\. Employee Engagement \& Advocacy**


* **Raise Awareness:** Partner with HR and plant managers to educate our workforce on how to contribute to the fund and how to apply for support.
* **In\-Person Outreach:** Conduct site visits, deliver presentations, and host employee meetings to build trust and drive engagement across all locations.

  




**What You Bring to the Team**
------------------------------


### **Qualifications \& Experience**


* **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in Nonprofit Management, Business Administration, Human Resources, or a closely related field preferred.
* **Experience:** Proven experience managing charitable programs, foundation grants, or corporate employee assistance funds (**required**).
* **Global Exposure:** Demonstrated ability to implement initiatives or lead programs across global, multi\-site, or newly acquired organizational footprints.
* **Financial Acumen:** Strong program administration skills with experience in budgeting, financial reporting, and donor tracking.

  




### **Key Competencies**


* **Strategic Leadership:** Ability to think long\-term and transition ideas into highly structured, compliant global programs.
* **Empathetic Communication:** Exceptional relationship\-building skills to connect with plant floor employees, executive leadership, and board members alike.
* **Nonprofit Governance:** Deep understanding of regulatory compliance, IRS guidelines for charitable funds, and board administration.

  




**Metrics for Success**
-----------------------


* **Growth** in active employee participation and total recurring contributions.
* **Awareness** increases across all manufacturing and corporate sites.
* **Efficiency** in processing and distributing critical grants equitably.
* **Seamless onboarding** of global sites and newly acquired locations into the program.

  




**Travel \& Physical Requirements**
-----------------------------------


* **Travel:** Ability to travel **20–25%** to various corporate and manufacturing locations to promote fund awareness and build community relationships.

Equal Opportunity Employer/Protected Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities  

This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights (https://www.eeoc.gov/poster) notice from the Department of Labor.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Advancement Services
jobs.description:
Shirley Ryan AbilityLab is the global leader in physical medicine and rehabilitation for adults and children with the most severe, complex conditions. By joining our team, you’ll be part of our life\-changing mission and vision. You’ll contribute to an innovative, multifaceted culture that is second to none — one that embraces collaboration, excellence, discovery and compassion. You’ll play a role in something that’s never been done before as we integrate science and clinical care to help patients achieve better, faster outcomes — as we Advance Human Ability, together.

**Job Description Summary**


The Executive Director, Advancement Services provides strategic leadership and oversight for the infrastructure, systems, data governance, analytics, and operational teams that enable a high\-performing, donor\-centered, and data\-driven advancement program. This role will serve as the primary leader and part of the advancement leadership team, focused on building a scalable advancement operations that support philanthropic growth, campaign readiness, and exceptional donor experience.  

  

The Executive Director leads a multi\-functional team responsible for advancement communications and stewardship, operations and data systems, direct response programs, and events. This position sets direction, establishes priorities, and ensures effective execution through team leadership, strong performance management, and cross\-functional alignment.  

  

The Executive Director will consistently demonstrate support of the Shirley Ryan AbilityLab statement of Vision, Mission and Core Values by striving for excellence, contributing to the team efforts and showing respect and compassion for patients and their families, fellow employees, and all others with whom there is contact at or in the interest of the institute.  

  

The Executive Director will demonstrate Shirley Ryan AbilityLab Core Attributes: Communication, Accountability, Flexibility/Adaptability, Judgment/Problem Solving, Customer Service and Core Values (Hope, Compassion, Discovery, Collaboration, and Commitment to Excellence) while fulfilling job duties.**Job Description**

**Principal Responsibilities**

* Establish and lead a comprehensive advancement services strategy that supports institutional priorities and fundraising goals.
* Provide strategic oversight and direction across advancement services functions, including donor relations and stewardship, advancement communications, operations and data systems, prospect management and research, direct response, events, gift processing, reporting and analytics, and data governance.
* Lead, mentor, and develop a high\-performing team of advancement services professionals through effective talent management, succession planning, coaching, and performance accountability.
* Oversee direct response and events functions as direct reports, ensuring strategic alignment, effective execution, a reliable pipeline of donors, and measurable return on investment.
* Ensure that donor relations, stewardship, and advancement communications programs deliver a cohesive, high\-quality, and donor\-centered experience.
* Direct the development and optimization of advancement systems, including the CRM (Blackbaud), ensuring data integrity, usability, and adoption.
* Oversee gift management and processing to ensure accuracy, timeliness, and compliance with institutional policies and regulatory requirements.
* Ensure strong prospect management and research practices that support pipeline development and frontline fundraising success.
* Champion the use of data and analytics to inform decision\-making, measure performance, and identify opportunities for growth.
* Establish, monitor, and continuously refine key performance indicators and dashboards across advancement services functions; provide actionable insights to drive growth and performance.
* Promote operational efficiency, scalability, and continuous improvement across processes, systems, and workflows.
* Partner closely with frontline fundraising leadership to ensure advancement services functions effectively support donor strategy and engagement.
* Ensure alignment and collaborative partnerships across the enterprise, specifically the finance and information technology departments.

**Reporting Relationships**


Reports to: Senior Vice President, Chief Advancement Officer


Direct reports include Advancement services team members across multiple functional areas including direct response and events. The team structure may evolve as the program grows.

**Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities Required**

* Strong understanding of fundraising metrics, campaign operations, donor lifecycle management, and advancement performance measurement.
* Knowledge of data governance, information security principles, and regulatory requirements related to donor information management.
* Ability to lead organizational change initiatives, technology implementations, and process transformation efforts.
* Strong strategic and systems\-oriented thinking with the ability to translate vision into execution through teams
* Demonstrated ability to lead, develop, and manage high\-performing teams
* Deep understanding of advancement operations, including CRM systems, data governance, prospect management, and donor engagement practices
* Ability to leverage data and analytics to inform strategy and drive results
* Excellent organizational, project management, and problem\-solving skills
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams
* Demonstrated commitment to operational excellence, innovation, digital transformation, change management, and continuous improvement

**Education and Experience**

* Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred
* Minimum of 8\+ years of progressively responsible leadership experience in advancement services, development operations, fundraising operations, or related nonprofit management function

jobs.title: Development Director
jobs.description:
Geneva Lake Conservancy is seeking a strategic, relationship\-driven Development Director to lead and grow a comprehensive fundraising program at a pivotal moment for land and water conservation in Walworth County and beyond.

Since 1977, GLC has protected the glacial lakes, forests, wetlands, working farmlands, and scenic corridors of Walworth County and its surrounding area. Today, we protect 3,745 acres through conservation easements, land ownership, and partnerships, while engaging the community through public nature preserves and programs such as Conservation@Home and Keep It Blue.

This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced fundraising leader to build lasting donor relationships, strengthen board and community engagement, and help scale annual contributed revenue from approximately $1 million toward $2–3 million over the next five years.

**In this role, you will:**

* Lead a multi\-year fundraising strategy aligned with GLC’s mission, growth goals, and conservation priorities.
* Manage and grow a portfolio of major donors, including cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of five\- and six\-figure gifts.
* Partner with the Executive Director and Board members on donor strategy, prospect development, and fundraising committee engagement.
* Oversee grants, foundation and corporate partnerships, fundraising events, donor communications, and development operations.
* Supervise and support one full\-time development staff member while helping foster a positive, accountable, mission\-driven team culture.

**We’re looking for someone who brings:**

* 5–7\+ years of progressive nonprofit fundraising experience, with demonstrated success securing major gifts.
* Strong relationship\-building skills and comfort working with donors, board members, volunteers, staff, and community partners.
* Experience developing fundraising plans, managing donor pipelines, writing grants, and using CRM systems to track progress and results.
* Clear written and verbal communication skills, sound judgment, and a collaborative leadership style.
* Interest in land conservation, environmental stewardship, community impact, or mission\-driven nonprofit work.

COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS

* Salary range: $85,000–$110,000, commensurate with experience.
* Benefits include up to 3% employer retirement match, monthly healthcare stipend, cell phone stipend, paid time off, and professional development.

HOW TO APPLY  
Interested candidates should submit a resume and cover letter describing their fundraising experience, leadership approach, and interest in Geneva Lake Conservancy’s mission. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, with priority consideration given to materials received by August 14, 2026\. Please send application materials to Executive Director, Ashley Hatley Caruso at careers@genevalakeconservancy.org.

ABOUT GENEVA LAKE CONSERVANCY  
Since 1977, Geneva Lake Conservancy has protected the glacial lakes, forests, wetlands, working farmlands, and scenic corridors of Walworth County. Today, GLC protects 3,745 acres through conservation easements, land ownership, and partnerships, and engages residents and visitors through six public nature preserves and community programs such as Conservation@Home and Keep It Blue. GLC was named the 2020 Land Trust of the Year by Gathering Waters Wisconsin.

Pay: $85,000\.00 \- $110,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 403(b)
* 403(b) matching
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer -The Linden Lodge Foundation
jobs.description:
Career Opportunity

The Linden Lodge Foundation seeks a Chief Executive Officer to preserve its extraordinary resident\-centered culture while strengthening the organizational capacity needed for long\-term sustainability. The next leader will build upon a foundation of exceptional care, trusted relationships with families, and a deeply committed staff while expanding fundraising, community visibility, governance, and operational excellence.

Mission: The Linden Lodge Foundation, Inc. is dedicated to the recovery of men and women with a serious and persistent mental illness by providing opportunities for our residents to live, work, and learn, while contributing their talents through a community of mutual support. We do this through providing a safe, "home\-like" residence and the development of innovative motivational, educational and vocational prospects.

Our goal is to provide a high quality of life for all residents, ongoing improvement and increased self\-esteem and the ultimate elimination of stigma against those with a mental illness. Residents, staff and volunteers work side\-by\-side to develop treatment plans, set goals and discover ways to successfully realize them. Success depends upon our ability to inspire partnerships between residents, staff, board members, volunteers, organization partners, medical providers and the larger community.

Reporting Structure: The Chief Executive Officer of the Foundation reports to the Board of Directors. The Chief Executive Officer provides strategic leadership while ensuring operational excellence, financial stewardship, staff development, and community engagement remain aligned with the organization's mission.

Total Staff and Budget: Eight staff members and an annual budget of $400,000\.

Salary: Base salary of $100,000 with a performance‑based bonus tied to fundraising results.

Working Environment/Location: Onsite in Aberdeen, North Carolina.

* Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, compassion, and continuous improvement.
* Guide organizational change while honoring the legacy established by the organization's founder.
* Ensure resident well\-being remains central to organizational decision\-making.

Fund Development

* Expand grant opportunities and diversify revenue sources.
* Establish measurable fundraising goals and regularly report progress.

Program Development and Resident Engagement

* Ensure residents receive recovery\-oriented, person\-centered support in a safe, stable, and home\-like environment.
* Strengthen structured daily activities, life\-skills programming, transportation, outings, and resident engagement systems.
* Work with staff to create meaningful opportunities for residents to build confidence, independence, connection, and purpose.
* Ensure program practices reflect resident dignity, choice, safety, and appropriate support for adults living with serious mental illness.
* Evaluate program quality and resident engagement on an ongoing basis and recommend improvements as needs evolve.

Operations \& Finance

* Provide oversight of budgeting, financial planning, and fiscal stewardship.
* Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and sound internal controls.
* Strengthen organizational policies, procedures, and administrative systems.
* Oversee human resources, technology, and risk management.
* Monitor organizational performance using meaningful metrics.

Board Relations

* Serve as the Board's chief strategic advisor.
* Provide timely reports and recommendations to support governance.
* Assist with strategic planning and policy development.
* Support committee effectiveness and Board engagement.
* Maintain open, transparent communication with Board leadership.

Community Leadership

* Serve as Linden Lodge's primary ambassador.
* Develop partnerships with behavioral health providers and community organizations.
* Increase public awareness of Linden Lodge's mission and impact.
* Represent the organization before civic, philanthropic, and governmental audiences.
* Strengthen relationships with families, volunteers, donors, and community leaders.

The Ideal Candidate Profile

The ideal candidate will possess the following qualifications:

Required Qualifications

* Progressive nonprofit leadership experience.
* Experience supervising and developing professional staff.
* Strong financial management and budgeting experience.
* Demonstrated fundraising and major gift experience.
* Demonstrated success working with governing boards.
* Excellent communication and relationship\-building skills.
* Bachelor’s degree preferably in psychology or social work.

Preferred Qualifications

* Behavioral health, residential services, or human services leadership experience.
* Grant development experience.
* Community partnership development experience.
* Master's degree or equivalent leadership experience preferred.

**Job Responsibilities**
------------------------


**Spartanburg Community College (SCC) seeks a dynamic, visionary, and results\-driven Executive Director of the SCC Foundation to lead a modern advancement enterprise focused on major gifts, capital investment, and workforce\-aligned philanthropy.**  

  

**The Executive Director serves as the College’s chief fundraising strategist and external ambassador, responsible for building transformative partnerships that advance SCC’s mission—particularly in workforce development, economic mobility, and regional prosperity. This leader will guide comprehensive fundraising efforts including capital campaigns, major and planned gifts, corporate partnerships, and strategic initiatives that strengthen both the institution and the students it serves.**  

  

**Reporting to Vice President of Community Impact for SCC, and managing a team of Foundation professionals, the successful candidate will collaborate closely with the Chairman of the Foundation Board, Foundation Board of Directors, and critical external and internal resources to help the Foundation accomplish its mission of supporting Spartanburg Community College. The immediate objectives of the Executive Director will be to expand philanthropic impact, elevate community visibility, and align funding strategies with high\-demand workforce programs, facilities, and innovation while supporting the ongoing needs of the students and the Foundation.**  

  

Strategic Fundraising \& Capital Development :  

Lead and execute a comprehensive fundraising strategy with emphasis on major gifts, capital campaigns, and endowment growth.  

Design and implement campaigns aligned with workforce development priorities, including new programs, workforce training facilities, and innovation initiatives.

Expand unrestricted and flexible funding to address emerging institutional needs.  

Implement best practices across the full donor lifecycle: qualification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship.  

  

Workforce Development \& Industry Partnerships:  

 Serve as a key connector between SCC and the regional business and industry community, aligning philanthropy with workforce needs.  

 Develop and steward strategic partnerships with employers, industry leaders, and economic development organizations.  

 Secure funding for high\-demand workforce programs, apprenticeships, credentials, and career pathways.  

 Collaborate with college leadership to position SCC as a leading workforce development engine in the region.

Build relationships with business leaders, government officials, alumni, and philanthropic organizations.  

Increase SCC’s regional and statewide visibility through strategic outreach, partnerships, and storytelling.  

Identify and pursue public\-private partnerships and grant opportunities.  

  

Board Relations \& Governance:

Engage board members as active partners in fundraising, advocacy, and community engagement.  

Support board development, recruitment, and ongoing engagement  

  

Organizational Leadership \& Operations

Spartanburg Community College is an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer for all qualified persons regardless of race, color, sex, including the basis of pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions, national origin, age, religion, or disability.

jobs.description:
**About ROCKBlue**

ROCKBlue is an international nonprofit organization committed to strengthening water and sanitation utilities so they can deliver reliable, affordable, and sustainable services to the communities they serve. Founded on the belief that durable improvements in water and sanitation come from strong institutions \- not one‑off projects \- ROCKBlue focuses on leadership, governance, performance, and financial sustainability at the utility level.

ROCKBlue works primarily across Africa, partnering directly with utility boards, senior management teams, development partners, and private‑sector actors. Its approach combines long‑term relationships, practical problem‑solving, and results‑driven accountability to help utilities move from chronic underperformance to stable, investable, and well‑governed institutions.

ROCKBlue delivers its mission through a mix of structured capacity‑building programs, peer‑to‑peer utility partnerships, targeted technical assistance, and performance‑oriented advisory services. The organization emphasizes measurable outcomes, leadership development, and operational discipline to ensure that improvements endure beyond the period of external support.

**ROCKBlue Consultancy** is the organization’s fee‑based advisory and professional services arm. It provides specialized technical, strategic, financial, and operational support to utilities, governments, development finance institutions, donors, and private‑sector clients. Revenues generated through the consultancy contribute to ROCKBlue’s financial sustainability while reinforcing and scaling its mission and impact.

**Co‑Managing Director Position Description**

ROCKBlue is seeking two Co‑Managing Directors (Co‑MDs) for the ROCKBlue Consultancy. The Co‑MDs jointly provide strategic, operational, and commercial leadership for the consultancy division, sharing responsibilities to ensure continuity, resilience, and consistent performance. This dual‑leadership model ensures that if one Co‑MD is unavailable or transitions out of the role, the consultancy retains strong, uninterrupted leadership.

Reporting to the President, the Co‑MDs collectively oversee strategic direction, client engagement, and business growth while ensuring that all consultancy activities align with ROCKBlue’s mission of strengthening water and sanitation utilities in underserved communities.

The Co‑MDs work closely together and in direct collaboration with international partners such as the development financial institutions, aid agencies, engineering firms, and nonprofits in the WASH sector. Together, they lead a team of highly skilled professionals, fostering innovation, operational excellence, and global impact.

As core members of ROCKBlue’s senior leadership, the Co‑MDs contribute jointly to organizational strategy, and business development, working closely with the President and CEO to expand ROCKBlue’s reach. This position offers a unique opportunity for shared leadership, cross‑sector collaboration, and real‑world impact on urban water and sanitation solutions.

**Purpose of the Co‑Managing Director Positions**

Together, the Co‑Managing Directors:

* Serve as the senior management leadership of the ROCKBlue Consultancy, jointly responsible for leading an effective, high‑performing, and financially sustainable consultancy.
* Agree amongst themselves on the allocation of tasks resulting from their position.
* Support the President in defining the consultancy’s strategic vision and are jointly accountable for its implementation and results.
* Set and model the consultancy’s ethical, professional, and operational standards in alignment with ROCKBlue’s long‑term strategic objectives.
* Act as joint spokespersons for the ROCKBlue Consultancy on the policies and objectives agreed with the President.
* Represent the consultancy to clients, partners, and other stakeholders, actively listening to, promoting, and advancing the consultancy’s work.

The Co‑MDs are expected to coordinate closely, clearly allocate responsibilities between themselves, and ensure transparency and continuity in decision‑making.

**Responsibilities (Shared Between the Co‑Managing Directors)**

**Strategic Leadership \& Business Development**With the help of the business development team members, jointly drive business efforts, ensuring revenue growth, client acquisition, and successful execution of contracts while maintaining high service quality.

**Financial \& Risk Oversight**Collectively ensure transparent financial management, oversee budgeting and reporting, and implement risk mitigation strategies to safeguard the consultancy’s operational and financial stability.

**Operational \& HR Optimization**Establish and maintain efficient organizational structures, strengthen performance management frameworks, and implement policies to attract, retain, and develop team members.

**Innovation \& Growth Strategy**Lead the development of new funding streams, enhance client confidence through effective contract execution, and implement strategies to drive long‑term sustainability and growth of the consultancy.

**Strategic Planning \& Execution**

* Lead ROCKBlue Consultancy's strategy to ensure mission fulfillment through planning, outreach, and effective operational policies.
* Develop annual divisional plans (OGSMs), secure approval, and jointly ensure successful implementation.

**Performance \& Accountability**

* Emphasize results over activities, using effective weekly tracking mechanisms.
* Ensure team accountability through clear delegation, motivation, and follow‑through.

**Systems \& Data Management**

Ensure efficient divisional database/CRM and knowledge‑management systems.

**Team Leadership \& Development**

* Recruit and retain the required team members.
* Delegate responsibilities appropriately between staff and specialists and amongst themselves
* Provide timely guidance, feedback, and performance management (including IQOs).

Description:

The Fund Development Director is responsible for the Girl Scouts Spirit of Nebraska’s fundraising efforts by managing a professional portfolio, with working knowledge of all areas within development including major gifts, annual giving, corporate and foundation gifts, grant and report writing, campaigns and appeals, data research and leading special events. The Fund Development Director serves as the strategic advisor to the CEO and CDO to position them best for fundraising efforts.

**Key Accountabilities:**


1\. Develops and maintains an extensive portfolio, expanding support from individuals, corporations, organizations, and foundations donors.


2\. Cultivates new donor relationships, maintains current donors, and re\-engages lapsed donors.


3\. Leads the management of grants and reports, including researching, identifying, and tracking grant progress, overseeing major gifts, and maintaining grant and report schedules.


4\. Maximizes fundraising opportunities to achieve financial and strategic goals.


5\. Networks within the community and stays current on local, regional, and national events.


6\. Manage all donor requests such as coordinating behind the scenes experiences and tours.


7\. Leads the planning and execution of giving campaigns and community partnerships.


8\. Develops and oversees strategies for major special events, including our signature event, Juliette Gordon Low Dinner, Cookie \& Beverage Parties, and Alumni Events, ensuring sponsorships and logistical coordination.


9\. Oversees the development and management of the fund development department and fundraising budgets, and reporting fundraising results, including income and expenses.


10\. Provide support to the Chief Development Officer and manages the Fund Development Manager, and Fund Development Coordinator.


11\. All other duties as assigned.


Requirements:
* Must reside in Omaha.
* Start date: No earlier than September 15, 2026\.
* Bachelor’s degree in a related field required.
* Minimum of five years of experience in nonprofit fund development, including demonstrated success in donor cultivation, solicitation, and fundraising initiatives.
* Experience with capital campaigns, grant writing, report writing, event leadership, and managing fundraising programs.
* Strong financial and budget management skills, including the ability to develop, monitor, and report on fundraising income and expenses.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to establish and maintain effective relationships with staff, volunteers, donors, and the public.
* Excellent organizational, planning, and time management skills, with the ability to prioritize, delegate, manage multiple projects simultaneously, meet deadlines, and maintain attention to detail.
* Ability to work effectively under pressure, solve problems, take initiative, and work independently with a high degree of integrity.

Founded in 2013 by family members who lost loved ones in the Sandy Hook Elementary School tragedy, Sandy Hook Promise is a national nonprofit organization dedicated to preventing gun violence and other forms of violence before they occur. Through evidence\-informed programs, bipartisan advocacy, research, and community engagement, the organization has trained millions of youth and adults to recognize warning signs, intervene early, and create safer schools and communities.  

Today, Sandy Hook Promise reaches more than 50 million participants nationwide and continues to expand its impact through innovative prevention programs, policy initiatives, and strategic partnerships.  

  

**Position Summary**  

Reporting to Co\-Founder and Chief Executive Officer Nicole Hockley, the Chief Development Officer (CDO) serves as a member of the Executive Leadership Team and provides strategic leadership for all fundraising activities across Sandy Hook Promise and the Sandy Hook Promise Action Fund.  

The CDO will lead the organization's next phase of philanthropic growth by developing and executing a comprehensive fundraising strategy that strengthens and diversifies revenue, deepens donor engagement, builds a high\-performing development team, and aligns fundraising efforts with the organization's strategic priorities. This executive will partner closely with organizational leadership, the Board of Directors, and external stakeholders to advance Sandy Hook Promise's mission and long\-term sustainability.

jobs.description:
**Executive Assistant to the CEO**

Miami, FL \| Full\-Time, On\-Site \| Nonprofit Music Education

www.YoungMusiciansUnite.org

**About Young Musicians Unite**

Established in 2013, Young Musicians Unite (YMU) is a Miami\-based nonprofit dedicated to ensuring every young person has access to music education, regardless of socioeconomic background. We partner with schools to provide free music programming that inspires personal development, builds creative and collaborative skills, and prepares students to become community leaders.

YMU supports over 16,000 students across more than 100 schools. We are a fast\-moving, innovative organization, constantly evolving, thinking outside the box, and raising the bar for what's possible in music education. Transforming Lives \& Communities Through Music.

**Position Overview**

The Executive Assistant to the CEO is a high impact role at the heart of YMU’s operations. As the CEO’s right hand, you will help keep priorities moving, communications organized, projects on track, and the calendar running seamlessly. You will serve as a central connector across the organization, coordinating with staff, board members, donors, sponsors, community partners, and other key stakeholders.

This is not a traditional Executive Assistant role. You will support many of the organization’s most important initiatives and relationships, helping ensure that ideas become action and commitments become results. The ideal candidate is proactive, highly organized, detail oriented, and comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast paced environment. They anticipate needs, communicate with professionalism and warmth, follow through consistently, and take ownership of projects from start to finish.

If you enjoy building relationships, solving problems, coordinating complex projects, and contributing to a mission driven organization with visible community impact, this role is for you.

**Position Details**

**Title:** Executive Assistant to the CEO

**Department:** Executive Office

**Reports To:** CEO

**Type:** Full\-Time, On\-Site

**Location:** Miami, FL

**Salary:** $50,500 – $55,500 (commensurate with experience)

**Key Responsibilities**

**Executive Assistant — CEO Support**

* Manage the CEO’s calendar, travel, communications, and meeting logistics, ensuring priorities are aligned and time is used effectively.
* Prepare meeting agendas, briefing materials, presentations, and follow up items to ensure the CEO is consistently prepared and informed.
* Track CEO priorities, monitor progress on key initiatives, and follow up with staff and stakeholders to keep projects moving forward.
* Capture and distribute meeting notes, action items, and decisions, ensuring accountability and timely execution.
* Process CEO expense reports and coordinate with Finance to maintain accurate records and documentation.
* Serve as the primary point of coordination between the CEO and internal and external stakeholders, facilitating communication, scheduling, and follow through.

**Development Support**

* Serve as the liaison between the CEO and the Development team, helping coordinate communication and follow up with donors, sponsors, and prospective supporters.
* Manage the events management platform for every ticketed event, from initial setup through final reconciliation
* Build each event in the system: create the event, set ticket tiers and pricing, and configure registration forms
* Track registrations, ticket sales, and payments in real time, and flag discrepancies before they become problems
* Maintain accurate guest lists, table assignments, and attendee records throughout the event lifecycle
* Document each event's full history in the platform, so every ticketed event has a complete, searchable record after it closes
* Support donor communications and campaign mailouts
* Maintain a real time fundraising dashboard tracking progress against campaign goals for leadership review

**Board and Committees**

* Work closely with the CEO to support the Executive Board, Community Board, Community Relations Committee, and Executive Committee
* Support the Gala Committee and Community Board event committees: prepare agendas, briefings, and presentations, and track action items
* Close the loop on every committee cycle: agendas go out before the meeting, recaps go out after, and every donor and committee member receives a timely thank you
* Draft communications to board and committee members for CEO review and distribution
* Maintain the board and committee directory as the organization's single source of truth for contact information and rosters

**What We're Looking For**

**Required**

* Bachelor's degree and a minimum of 2 years of professional experience supporting a C\-suite executive or senior organizational leader
* High attention to detail and the ability to consistently produce accurate work.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills — you write clearly, professionally, and with warmth
* Ability to anticipate needs, take initiative, and follow through with minimal supervision.
* Ability to work a flexible schedule that may include occasional evening and weekend events.
* Discretion and sound judgment when handling sensitive donor, board, and financial information
* Passionate about music education and the communities YMU serves
* Proficiency in Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Forms) — strong emphasis on Sheets and Drive
* Experience with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel)
* Experience with Canva
* Experience with AI productivity tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, or similar platforms.

FSU Division of University Advancement


Florida State University is currently in the leadership phase of a comprehensive campaign. As the leadership phase of the campaign begins, the Division of University Advancement is accelerating the hiring of additional development officers’ campus\-wide and is excited to build out its team. For Advancement professionals who are interested in being a part of a comprehensive campaign from the beginning for their career development, the timing is ideal to join our team.

The Senior Director of Development strategically designs and executes a personal solicitation program resulting in philanthropic support through the identification, cultivation, solicitation and stewardship of high\-level prospects for the Herbert Wertheim College of Business. The Senior Director of Development secures, on an annual basis, private support at the major gift level ($100,000\) while also identifying, developing and managing a portfolio primarily consisting of $1,000,000 and above donors. Serving as the chief strategist, develops, oversees and coordinates the fundraising plans of the College with the Dean and the Associate Vice President, Colleges, Schools and Units. Prepares prospect materials for the President of the University, the Vice President, the Associate Vice President, the Dean and other senior administration as appropriate to a prospect’s situation.

jobs.description:
**Who We Are**

Outlook Advisory Group (OAG) provides Fractional COO services for law firms and is a trusted consulting partner to organizations across the legal sector. We deliver high\-level advisory services that drive performance, support sustainable growth, and elevate operational integrity. We operate in the space between small, founder\-led operations and law firms who have grown large enough to hire a full executive suite. Our typical clients are small\- to mid\-size law firms who are eager to add efficiency and scale with a commitment to doing things differently. Based in Charlotte, NC and serving clients virtually nationwide, we provide a tailored approach to each engagement, diving deep with law firm Owners/Founders/Managing Partners on strategic planning, business advisory, leadership development, process design and improvement, data and metrics, and discerning implementation of key initiatives. Our clients stay with us for the long\-term, typically 2\+ years, so it’s important to build unparalleled relationships and trust embedded in the client’s organization.

We are committed to taking the burden off the shoulders of law firm owners so they can reclaim their time and return to visionary, strategic work. Operating as true partners within our clients’ firms, we guide our client organizations to be the best they can be, bridging the gap between high\-level strategy, day\-to\-day operations, and complex technology and processes. OAG cuts through backend chaos to establish clear workflows, from software optimization to HR compliance. We are mission\-driven and operate with a high level of integrity and as trusted advisors. We are committed to results and to building a connected culture.

**Who We Want**

We are seeking a Fractional COO Consultant with a strong background in law firm/legal operations at the executive level, with knowledge of law firm\-specific processes, language, and liability prevention. Preferably, the COO Consultant has experience scaling a firm for growth and optimizing their operational playbook. Our ideal candidate is strategic, calm, discerning, motivational, fast\-paced, tech\-forward, and a trusted advisor with comfort interfacing with CEOs and Founders at an executive level.

This is a hands\-on role shaping approximately 4 to 7 law firms at a time via dedicated strategy and implementation, leveraging the firm’s internal resources as well as technology and excellent time management and prioritization. Being a fractional executive provides the opportunity to guide multiple organizations simultaneously. Our team is low\-ego and committed to constant learning and improvement.

**Responsibilities**

* Maintain excellent client communication and develop strong, trusting relationships with clients.
* Positively represent our organization and brand with clients, in the community, and online.

* Attend 1\-2 monthly virtual roundtable events for existing and potential clients to discuss topics and trends in law firm operations.
* Participate in light marketing initiatives such as blog topic planning, video Q\&A, and presentations in the industry.

**Qualifications**

* At least a bachelor’s degree in Management, Operations, Accounting, Communications, or a related field; MBA preferred.
* At least 5 years of experience in law firm(s); with at least 2 in an executive leadership role.
* Knowledge and understanding of different law firm practice areas, billing structures, and administrative setups.
* Solution\-focused, and non\-judgmental approach to client work.
* High level of professionalism, including while working remotely, with elevated business style of dress and polished presentation to clients, potential clients, and vendors.
* Willingness to be an additional face of the brand and public ambassador.

**Why Outlook Advisory Group**

At Outlook Advisory Group, you’ll have the opportunity to build something great from the ground up. As a rapidly scaling organization, you will benefit from direct contact and collaboration/mentorship with the Founder and other team members. We are building and evolving our systems as the business grows, and you’ll have the opportunity to provide input and shape how we build. We expect those who invest in this role and our organization will experience a commensurate upside. We support continuous development and certifications, networking and being a connector in the industry, and a flexible schedule to meet client needs.

We offer health, vision, and dental insurance, 401(k), and a collaborative, supportive work environment where we work hard and also respect the needs and balance of our personal lives.

We are open to different work arrangements, whether standard W\-2 employment or full\-time or part\-time contractor setup, dependent on business and candidates’ mutual workload capacity and scheduling needs.

jobs.title: NWAC Executive Director
jobs.description:
**Details**
-----------


**The Northwest Athletic Conference (NWAC) invites applications and nominations for the position of Executive Director.**

**About NWAC:** The NWAC includes 37 community colleges across Washington, Oregon, and British Columbia. Member institutions compete for conference championships in men’s and women’s cross country, soccer, basketball, golf, and outdoor track, as well as volleyball, baseball, and softball. NWAC student\-athletes regularly continue their academic and athletic careers at four\-year institutions, with some advancing to professional competition.  

  

NWAC is a partnership of student\-centered community colleges with strong athletic traditions and a shared commitment to academic achievement, community service, and character development through athletics. The conference governs intercollegiate athletic programs for its member institutions and supports meaningful competitive opportunities for student\-athletes.

**What you'll do:** The NWAC Executive Director serves as the chief executive officer of the Northwest Athletic Conference. This position provides steady administrative leadership, oversees daily operations and staff, and implements the conference’s vision and strategic plan. The NWAC team consists of three full\-time and one part\-time staff. The Executive Director also ensures consistent application of conference bylaws, facilitates communication among member institutions, and oversees the planning and delivery of all NWAC\-sponsored activities.


The next Executive Director will be committed to balancing academic and athletic excellence, promoting character development and community service, and strengthening the student\-athlete experience. This leader will also bring a strategic vision that positions the NWAC as a strong, collaborative, and forward\-looking conference for years to come.


The Executive Director’s impact on the NWAC and its member institutions will focus on four key priorities: increasing the visibility of the conference and its members; strengthening the NWAC brand and reinforcing the positive reputation of member institutions; expanding engagement with alumni, media, and external partners; and supporting member institutions in achieving their strategic program goals by serving as the central coordinating force for intercollegiate competition across the conference.  

  

The Executive Director position is 40 hours per week and ineligible for overtime under FLSA. This position reports directly to the NWAC Executive Board. The salary range for this position is $160,000 to $175,000 and is not negotiable beyond this range. Salary and benefits are competitive and commensurate with qualifications and experience.  

  

**Check out the** **NWAC website** **to learn more** **about NWAC!**  

  

**Applications will be reviewed after the posting closes on August 11, 2026\. We anticipate a start date of December 1, 2026\.**  


**Example of Duties \& Responsibilities**
-----------------------------------------


* Provides leadership for the NWAC including strategic planning, development, implementation and evaluation of programs, and all functions of the organization.
* Provides the NWAC Executive Board and commission accurate, timely, and relevant information necessary to promote the goals and objectives of the Conference and NWAC athletics.
* Works with the NWAC executive board in the development of goals for the Conference and the Executive Director.
