jobs.company: National Trust for Historic Preservation in the United States
jobs.title: Chief of Staff to the President & CEO
jobs.description:
**JOB SUMMARY**


The Chief of Staff to the President \& CEO of the National Trust for Historic Preservation plays a crucial role in establishing the Office of the President. The Chief of Staff will support the President in all their executive duties, including facilitating efficient and effective day to day engagement, managing important administrative and planning functions, coordinating high impact thought leadership and public advocacy, and advancing relationship\-building with funders and external partners.


The Chief of Staff will act as the President’s representative and advocate both internally and externally, working to advance their agenda in building new strategic partnerships, facilitating cross\-functional projects within the organization, and project managing targeted special initiatives at the President’s direction. The Chief of Staff will also lead and build the infrastructure of the President’s office itself. This will involve coordinating with the executive team and all divisions of the National Trust to support the planning, tracking, and implementation of the President’s priorities, as well as leading the President’s strategic engagement with the Board of Trustees.


Managing the Office of the President involves supervising a team that includes the Director of Presidential Office Communications and an Executive Assistant. This role requires the development of processes and tools to ensure effective coordination both within the Office of the President and across the divisions and subsidiaries of the National Trust. The ideal candidate for this executive position will be dynamic, highly organized, and an effective communicator. They should possess a diverse skill set and will play a crucial role as a key member of the President’s executive team. It is important to note that this role is distinct from that of a Chief Operating Officer. The new President \& CEO will be responsible for recruiting a Chief Operating Officer within the next six months.

***(A Resume and a Cover Letter are Required for this role.)***

**DUTIES**

**Support day\-to\-day activities of the President \& CEO**

* Assist the President by overseeing daily tasks and managing information flow. Act as the primary contact point, both internally and externally, to triage incoming requests for the President’s engagement and maximize the effective use of the President’s time.
* Serve as a representative of the President as needed, both internally and externally. Proactively identify solutions and promote the President's agenda on their behalf, ensuring follow\-up to maintain progress on strategic priorities.
* Ensure the President’s preparedness for all engagements in coordination with the executive team, Director of Presidential Office Communications, and other project managers. This will involve creating, editing, and/or collating briefings, talking points, call sheets, and other documents while integrating data\-driven metrics.
* Act as a key thought partner to the President on strategic priorities, communications, and management strategies, in collaboration with the National Trust staff.
* Prepare the presentation materials for the President for executive team meetings, Board of Trustees meetings, NTHP all\-staff meetings, and other engagements. Collaborate with the Director of Presidential Office Communications to ensure that the presentations align with the overall messaging and to develop accompanying talking points.
* Staff the President as needed at events, meetings, and other in\-person engagements.

**Manage the Office of the President department**

* Build operations infrastructure for the Office of the President, including tools and processes for calendar managing, tracking priorities, overseeing departmental budgets, and facilitating other administrative activities.
* Structure and oversee management cadence to coordinate between the Office of the President and other critical functions (e.g., legal, finance, preservation, historic sites, development, marketing).
* Oversee management infrastructure to facilitate President’s coordination with the executive team. Set standing executive team meeting cadence and agendas, facilitate meeting discussion, and facilitate follow\-up including dissemination of notes.
* Supervise two direct reports: Director of Presidential Office Communications and the Executive Assistant of the President, and manage project coordination and deliverables, team building, and operational efficiencies for effective performance.
* Work closely with the future Chief Operating Officer and the executive team to align the Office of the President with strategic operational goals, procedures, and frameworks. Coordinate on change management, organizational culture, and reporting with Human Resources and the executive team.

**Oversee special projects at the direction of the President \& CEO**

* Scope, plan, and execute special projects and events owned by the Office of the President, at the direction of the President and in coordination with Office of the President staff and divisions across the National Trust.
* Engage consulting firms and assemble project teams that include both internal and external stakeholders to support Presidential priorities, such as developing an organizational Strategic Plan.
* Scope and launch new, cross\-functional initiatives with external partners, in coordination with divisions across the National Trust. Lead hand\-off of mature initiatives to divisional ownership, in coordination with members of the executive team.
* Join the President in partnership and fundraising relationship\-building. Serve as a primary point of contact for strategic external relationships on behalf of the President. Facilitate organizational engagement within the National Trust in response to external opportunities.
* Track metrics and assess impact of activities for reporting and updates to the President, including Board of Trustees engagement, speaking invitations and engag

jobs.title: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) – Drug & Alcohol Treatment Service
jobs.description:
**About the Role:**


Dunbar Wellness Group is seeking an experienced and visionary **Chief Executive Officer (CEO)** to lead our newly acquired Drug \& Alcohol Treatment organization in **Allentown, Pennsylvania**. The CEO will provide strategic, operational, and financial leadership while ensuring the organization delivers high\-quality, person\-centered substance use disorder treatment services in compliance with Pennsylvania Department of Drug and Alcohol Programs (DDAP), Department of Human Services (DHS), applicable federal regulations, and accreditation standards.


The ideal candidate is an accomplished healthcare executive with demonstrated experience leading behavioral health or substance use disorder organizations, driving organizational growth, maintaining regulatory compliance, and developing high\-performing leadership teams.

**Essential Duties \& Responsibilities**
----------------------------------------

### **Executive Leadership**

* Provide strategic leadership and operational oversight for all clinical, administrative, and financial operations.
* Develop and execute organizational goals aligned with the company's mission, vision, and long\-term strategic plan.
* Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, quality improvement, and client\-centered care.
* Lead and mentor executive leadership, clinical leadership, and administrative teams.

### **Operations \& Compliance**

* Ensure compliance with all applicable **Pennsylvania DDAP**, **DHS**, **CMS**, **Medicaid**, **HIPAA**, **42 CFR Part 2**, **OSHA**, and other federal and state regulations.
* Oversee licensing, accreditation, audits, quality assurance, and risk management activities.
* Develop and implement policies, procedures, and operational standards that promote regulatory compliance and organizational excellence.
* Monitor organizational performance through key performance indicators (KPIs) and quality improvement initiatives.

### **Financial Management**

* Oversee budgeting, financial planning, revenue cycle performance, and fiscal accountability.
* Monitor organizational financial health and identify opportunities for sustainable growth.
* Collaborate with finance and executive leadership to develop annual budgets and strategic investments.

### **Clinical \& Program Oversight**

* Partner with clinical leadership to ensure delivery of high\-quality, evidence\-based substance use disorder treatment services.
* Support program expansion and the development of new behavioral health and recovery initiatives.
* Ensure services remain compliant with payer requirements and clinical best practices.

### **Business Development**

* Build and maintain relationships with referral sources, healthcare partners, community organizations, government agencies, and stakeholders.
* Identify opportunities for growth through strategic partnerships, acquisitions, and new service lines.
* Represent the organization within the community and at professional events.

### **Human Resources \& Leadership**

* Oversee executive hiring, leadership development, succession planning, and organizational culture.
* Promote employee engagement, retention, and professional development.
* Collaborate with Human Resources on workforce planning and organizational initiatives.

**Minimum Qualifications**
--------------------------

### **Required**

* Master's degree in Healthcare Administration, Business Administration, Public Health, Social Work, Counseling, Psychology, or a related field.
* Minimum **10 years** of progressive leadership experience in behavioral health, healthcare, or substance use disorder treatment.
* Minimum **5 years** of executive or senior leadership experience.
* Demonstrated experience managing multi\-disciplinary healthcare operations.
* Strong knowledge of Pennsylvania behavioral health and substance use disorder regulations.
* Experience with budgeting, financial oversight, strategic planning, and organizational growth.
* Exceptional leadership, communication, and decision\-making skills.

### **Preferred**

* Licensed clinician (LPC, LCSW, LMFT, Psychologist, CRNP, Physician, etc.).
* Experience leading a DDAP\-licensed treatment organization.
* Experience with Joint Commission, CARF, or similar accreditation standards.
* Experience with mergers, acquisitions, or organizational integration.

**Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities**
----------------------------------

* Executive leadership and organizational management
* Healthcare regulatory compliance
* Financial and operational management
* Strategic planning and business development
* Change management
* Quality improvement
* Risk management
* Relationship building and stakeholder engagement
* Excellent written and verbal communication
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills

**Benefits**
------------

* Competitive Executive Compensation
* Performance\-Based Incentive Opportunities
* Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance
* Paid Time Off (PTO/VTO)
* Paid Holidays
* Professional Development \& Continuing Education
* Executive Leadership Development Opportunities

**Why Join Us?**
----------------


This is a unique opportunity to lead and shape the future of a growing substance use disorder treatment organization while making a lasting impact on individuals, families, and communities. As CEO, you will play a critical role in expanding access to quality treatment services, driving innovation, and building a culture of excellence.

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer (COO)
jobs.description:
**Our Company**



CRM is growing and we are looking for a few great employees who want to be part of a rewarding, fast\-paced and team\-orientated environment.

  

Founded in 2006, Cascade Receivables Management (“CRM”) is a leading financial services company that provides accounts receivable liquidity solutions to the consumer finance and healthcare industries. In addition to supporting the specialty finance activities of its sister company, Cascade Capital Funding, CRM’s suite of products and services include master servicing, third party collections, and revenue cycle optimization.

 **Position Overview**



The COO serves as the primary operational leader and strategic partner to the Chief Executive Officer and other C\-suite executives. While the CEO focuses on overall corporate vision, strategy, and high\-level growth, the COO is tasked with executing that vision. This role is responsible for overseeing the day\-to\-day operations of the debt purchase, specialty finance, and servicing divisions, ensuring best in class performance, maximum efficiency, rigid compliance, and seamless B2B client experiences within the healthcare and consumer finance sectors.

  



Own P\&L and compliance ledger for outsourced collections (agency and legal), in\-house collections, and specialized collections. Manage internal operations team, ensuring compliant and efficient business processes for inventory management, placements and recalls, putbacks, etc. Manage client services and IT systems teams, work with compliance and legal, finance and accounting, and other company stakeholders to effectuate positive change.

 **Supervisory Responsibilities**



Manage Outsourced Operations, including the outsourced vendor managers for both agency and legal networks, the Internal Operations team, the Client Services team, and IT Systems team.

 **Responsibilities**


* Manage IT Systems, Client Services teams.
* Manage and positively influence performance managers for both outsourced agency and legal recovery channels. Drive placement and performance strategy and oversee performance audits. Meet all compliance and business requirements managing external vendors, internal processes and staff. Meet monthly revenue and expense projections.
* Manage internal operations team, inventory management, systems and data integrity, placements, recalls, recourse/putbacks, client requests, reports including remits, demographic and status updates.
* Manage full scope internal collections efforts and strategy, onshore and off, omnichannel/digital and human. General collections and bankruptcy servicing.
* Onboard vendors and manage specialized vendor network including insurance discovery and outsourced billing, MVA, and other complex claims (medical) vendors.
* Stakeholder in credit committee and “deal desk” (purchase and investment decisioning).

  


**Qualifications**


**Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities**



Job\-Related Knowledge


* Thorough understanding of portfolio performance forecasting and budgeting tools and techniques.


Job\-Related Skills \& Abilities


* Employee oversight – hiring, training, performance evaluation and coaching, employee development skills
* Advanced PC and analytical skills.
* Strong negotiating skill, employee and vendor motivational skills.
* Exceptional written and verbal communication skills
* Ability to interface effectively across all levels of management and staff.

  


**Experience**


* **P\&L Ownership:** 10\+ years of P\&L ownership and senior level management experience in collections/accounts receivables management and/or debt buying industries with a focus on operations, analytics, client services, and IT development and implementations, outsourced and internal collections.
* **Change Management:** 5\+ years of experience implementing new technologies, restructuring workflows, and driving operational efficiency across both internal teams and external vendor networks.
* **Data Ingest and Integrity:** 5\+ years of experience overseeing the technical backbone required to ingest large data sets from institutional sellers and healthcare providers, ensuring data integrity.
* **Yield Optimization Analytics:** 5\+ years of experience building and using intelligence tools, defining and reporting to KPI’s, to track liquidation yield, cost to collect, streamline operations and monitor vendor performance.
* **Legal/Compliance:** Deep familiarity with Receivables Management Association International (RMAI) certification standards, as well as federal regulations like the FDCPA, FCRA, TCPA, and CFPB guidelines. Comprehensive understanding of HIPAA compliance, ability to learn Medicare/Medicaid guidelines, and complex payer requirements.

  


**Education**



Bachelor’s degree required. Advanced Degree in Management and/or Finance preferred.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Washington Student Achievement Council (WSAC)
jobs.description:
**Description**
---------------

  

  

**Please Note:** The starting salary offer will be determined based on the successful candidates’ qualifications within the starting salary range of $180,000 \- $231,813 annually. Our physical office is located in Olympia, WA. This is a hybrid position with the ability to telework. However, you will need to travel throughout Washington State to attend in\-person meetings or community events as required by the position.

**Terms of Appointment:** Per RCW, the Council shall employ an executive director. The executive director shall be appointed by the governor from a list of three names submitted by the Council. However, the governor may request, and the Council shall provide, an additional list or lists from which the governor shall select the executive director. The governor may dismiss the executive director only with the approval of a majority vote of the Council. The Council, by a majority vote, may dismiss the executive director.

 **Organization Summary**


The Washington Student Achievement Council (WSAC) is a cabinet\-level state agency responsible for expanding access to postsecondary education opportunities, advancing educational attainment and increasing student success for all Washingtonians. WSAC actively supports all pathways to postsecondary education, including Career and Technical Education (CTE) and apprenticeship programs.


Through strategic planning, policy leadership, research, advocacy, and program administration, the Washington Student Achievement Council (WSAC) serves as Washington's catalyst for educational attainment and workforce success. The Council is committed to advancing equitable pathways to education, training, and career advancement for all Washingtonians while fostering a shared statewide commitment to opportunity and economic mobility. By convening leaders across education, workforce, business, philanthropy, government, and community sectors, WSAC builds consensus, aligns strategy, and drives collective action to ensure that every learner has access to high\-quality pathways beyond high school and the support needed to thrive in a rapidly evolving economy.


WSAC administers and oversees a broad portfolio of programs and initiatives, including the Washington College Grant, State Work Study, College Bound Scholarship, GEAR UP, Washington's 529 college savings programs (GET and DreamAhead), Career Connect Washington, and a variety of college access and support programs. The agency also serves as a leading source of education research, data analysis, and policy recommendations that help shape the future of Washington's postsecondary education system. In addition, WSAC provides policy, research, and administrative support to several statewide statutory initiatives, including the independent Workforce Education Investment Accountability and Oversight Board (WEIAOB). Through this partnership, the agency helps advance statewide workforce education priorities while supporting legislative accountability and cross\-sector collaboration.


With over 150 employees and responsibility for approximately $590M in financial aid and education investments, WSAC plays a critical role in helping Washington meet its long\-term educational attainment and workforce development goals.

 **Position Summary**


The Executive Director serves as the Chief Executive Officer of the Washington Student Achievement Council and is responsible for leading the agency's strategic vision, policy agenda, operations, partnerships, and statewide impact.


This role represents a unique opportunity to shape the future of education in Washington State. The Executive Director serves as a trusted advisor to the Governor, the Council, legislators, educational leaders, and community partners while advancing policies and initiatives that increase educational attainment, improve affordability and access, strengthen workforce readiness, and create greater economic opportunity for Washingtonians.


The Executive Director leads one of Washington's most influential education agencies in an environment where collaboration is essential and formal authority is intentionally distributed. Because the state's K–12, higher education, and workforce systems are governed by multiple independent entities, success depends on the ability to build trust, align diverse stakeholders, shape policy through influence, and inspire collective action in pursuit of Washington's educational attainment and workforce goals.


Building upon a strong organizational foundation, this leader will guide the next chapter of WSAC's evolution as Washington’s leading voice for educational attainment, student success, and systems innovation. The Executive Director will bring together leaders from education, workforce, business, government, philanthropy, and community organizations to develop shared strategies, navigate complex policy challenges, and advance durable solutions that expand opportunity for all Washingtonians.


Through visionary leadership, strategic partnerships, and a steadfast commitment to equity, the Executive Director will ensure that WSAC's priorities reflect broad stakeholder engagement and are recognized as statewide commitments rather than the positions of any single agency or institution. In doing so, this leader will help shape a stronger future for Washington's students, families, employers, and communities.


Washington is at a pivotal moment in expanding educational opportunity, strengthening workforce readiness, and improving economic mobility. This is a rare opportunity to shape the future of educational attainment and workforce opportunity in Washington—creating lasting impact for students, families, employers, and communities while ensuring every Washingtonian has access to meaningful pathways beyond high school and the opportunity to thrive.


**Duties**
----------

**Strategic Leadership for Innovation**

* Serve as the Chief Executive Office

jobs.title: Director of Partnerships
jobs.description:
**About Breakthrough Discoveries**


Breakthrough Discoveries is a lean, high\-impact nonprofit dedicated to changing the trajectory of bipolar disorder research. We believe that by bridging the gap between innovative scientific inquiry and patient\-centered care, we can accelerate the next generation of interventions and create a world where all people with bipolar disorder thrive. We prioritize agility, transparency, and a steadfast focus on our mission.


**About the Director of Partnerships position**


We are looking for a dynamic Director of Partnerships to build and lead the fundraising engine that will fuel Breakthrough Discoveries' growth and drive innovation for mental health research. This role isn't just about closing gifts; it's about designing a development program from the ground up for a lean, mission\-driven organization. You will own the full arc of fundraising and partnership development strategy \- from pipeline to portfolio to stewardship \- ensuring our scientific partners have the resources they need to move fast and think big.


You will serve as a foundational member of our leadership team, shaping how we engage donors, build relationships, and scale impact. We expect you to be an exceptional leader who can build something from very little, move with urgency, and bring others along with you.


**Director of Partnerships responsibilities are:**


* **Fundraising Strategy:** Design and drive a comprehensive development strategy aligned to Breakthrough Discoveries' mission and growth targets. Evaluate and refine approaches continuously to meet short\- and long\-range goals.
* **Major Gifts:** Cultivate and manage an active portfolio of major and principal gift prospects. Engage directly in cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship across individuals, foundations, and corporations.
* **Pipeline Architecture:** Build a sustainable pipeline through organic inbound leads, prospect research, data analytics, community connection, and strategic outreach. Create the systems and processes that make development repeatable and scalable.
* **Donor Relations:** Lead a comprehensive stewardship program that deepens donor connection to Breakthrough Discoveries' work. Design acknowledgment processes and engagement strategies that recognize personal motivation and maximize long\-term donor potential.
* **Community:** Build community with change\-makers in bipolar research. Lead the development of high\-profile events to celebrate the progress in bipolar research. Work to develop action\-oriented strategy across the community of mental health funders.
* **Team and Infrastructure:** Select and implement software and tools to support development operations. Build toward an eventual development team.
* **Board and Leadership Engagement:** Build strong working relationships with board, advisory, and organizational leaders. Coordinate fundraising efforts to support the organizational vision and shape a compelling philanthropic case for support.
* **Compliance and Stewardship:** Coordinate with support teams to ensure regulatory and ethical compliance. Represent the organization at internal and external events.

**Who are You:**


* **A Builder:** You thrive in early\-stage environments. You see a blank slate and instinctively know how to construct something durable.
* **A Relationship Architect:** You understand that major philanthropy is built on trust, and you have the patience and presence to earn it.
* **Mission\-Aligned:** You have a genuine interest in scientific advancement and empathy for the community living with bipolar disorder.
* **Science Savvy:** You enjoy talking and learning about the science and clinical care of bipolar disorder and understand the environment of scientists, clinicians, and people with lived experience.
* **An Entrepreneurial Leader:** You know how to do a lot with a little, and you bring others along without losing momentum.
* **A Strategic Executor:** You can hold the big picture and manage the details — moving fluidly between vision and implementation.
* **Remote Native:** You are an expert at digital collaboration and can create meaningful connection from any distance.

**Director of Development requirements are:**


* 10\+ years of progressive fundraising experience, with a demonstrated track record in major gifts and campaign leadership, ideally within a scientific or health\-focused nonprofit environment.
* Solid experience across the full development function, including major and principal gifts, annual giving, corporate and foundation relations, stewardship, and donor communications.
* Strong practical knowledge of fundraising metrics, portfolio analytics, and performance tracking to inform strategy and report progress against goals.
* Working experience with CRM platforms and fundraising software; comfort adopting and onboarding new tools to support a growing development operation.
* Ability to understand and break down complex scientific and clinical jargon and speak to scientists and non\-scientists alike about the value of research.
* Exceptional organizational and leadership abilities, with experience managing or building teams and setting individual and collective fundraising goals.
* Outstanding communication and relationship skills, with the ability to engage credibly and warmly with donors, board members, scientific leaders, and internal colleagues at all levels.
* Familiarity with nonprofit legal and ethical guidelines governing fundraising, donor intent, fund stewardship, and gift acceptance policies.

**A Note on Location \& Work Eligibility:** To be considered for this role, you must currently reside in the U.S. and possess valid, ongoing work authorization that does not require company sponsorship.

**San Diego Youth Services****JOB ANNOUNCEMENT****Senior Director of Philanthropy**  

San Diego Youth Services seeks a Senior Director of Philanthropy in the Marketing \& Development program to be based in San Diego. This is a full\-time \& exempt position reporting to the Chief Executive Officer.


San Diego Youth Services: Who we are


San Diego Youth Services has helped improve the lives of more than 834,000 homeless, runaway and vulnerable youth since its beginning in 1970\. We provide safe places to live and long\-term solutions through shelter, foster homes, community centers and housing to more than 16,000 youth every year at 100\+ community and school locations. Through prevention, early intervention and treatment, we also help youth before they need higher levels of care or become homeless.


We aim to:

* Meet the basic needs of youth. Hunger, poverty and youth homelessness are on the rise for many youth and their families today. We help meet these basic needs for youth. What could be more vital than food and shelter? We provide safe places to live and long\-term solutions through shelter, foster homes, community centers and housing.
* Create positive connection. We help youth focus on positive life changes and healthy relationships as part of providing needed resources and services. San Diego Youth Services has long provided foster homes and adoption support services for families considering adopting a youth in the foster care system.
* Help youth develop life skills. In addition to a range of safe and stable housing options, we offer support to help youth develop the independent living skills necessary to become self\-sufficient and keep off the streets.
* Promote overall health and well\-being. Early intervention and care for the whole child are critical. We provide a broad continuum of services to help youth overcome trauma and challenges that put them at risk, including case management, counseling, mental health treatment and educational support and resources.


SDYS has a staff of approximately 250 employees and 80 volunteers working across San Diego County and an annual budget of approximately $24 million.

Breast cancer is a complex disease with no simple solution. Research is the key to stopping it in its tracks. Founded in 1993 by Evelyn H. Lauder, the Breast Cancer Research Foundation is the largest private funder of breast cancer research in the world. By investing in the best minds in science to examine every aspect of the disease from prevention to metastasis—and fostering cross\-disciplinary collaboration—BCRF is accelerating the entire field and moves us closer to the answers we urgently need to be the end of breast cancer.


**ABOUT THE ROLE**  

The Managing Director, Corporate Partnerships, Strategic Growth and Business Development is a unique and exciting new role at BCRF designed to secure new corporate partners for the organization. The position calls for a highly experienced, energetic fundraiser and team player with a strong passion for relationship\-building, an impressive record of developing and closing corporate nonprofit partnership agreements, and a commitment to generating new business. This role requires a dynamic individual who can confidently lead the BCRF corporate sales strategy from open to close, interface comfortably and effectively with senior executives and think creatively about BCRF assets and benefits to launch mutually beneficial partnerships.  

  

Reporting to the Chief Partnerships Officer, the Managing Director, Corporate Partnerships, Strategic Growth and Business Development will be responsible for creating and implementing an integrated strategy to elevate a comprehensive and sustainable corporate engagement sales program. The position would supervise two full\-time current team members and grow the Managing Director, Corporate Partnerships, Strategic Growth and Business Development staff to a team of at least five individuals within the next 5 years. This individual will accelerate BCRF’s annual and long\-term revenue growth, marketing and brand reach, and mission goals.  

  

This position is based in New York, New York. BCRF currently requires staff to work onsite a minimum of three days per week.  

  

Primary Responsibilities


* Develop, implement and drive a new business strategy to significantly increase corporate revenue.
* Research, identify, and track a strategic and sizeable pipeline of potential corporate partners who align with our brand and values, and create a robust, ongoing prospecting and cultivation plan for engaging prospective partners in the exploration of supporting BCRF.
* Lead negotiations with potential partners, ensuring mutually beneficial agreements are reached to close new partnerships.
* Oversee the refinement and development of sales positioning and collateral.
* Create customized, solutions\-based packages to generate new revenue and meet clients' needs.
* Deliver compelling presentations and pitches with passion and enthusiasm to motivate and inspire partners.

* Supervise and mentor direct reports, utilizing an empathetic and supportive leadership style to maintain a high level of performance across the department.

* Participate and assist in developing best practices, policy determination and management decisions of the Corporate Partnership team.

new_jobs.company: First Friends of New Jersey and New York
new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
**About the Organization and Role**

First Friends of New Jersey \& New York is a nonprofit organization dedicated to upholding the inherent dignity and humanity of immigrants who are impacted by the immigration enforcement system. We provide compassion and hope through visitation, accompaniment, material and post\-release support, advocacy, and community engagement. Our goal is to ensure that no one faces detention alone.

Founded nearly thirty years ago by volunteers, First Friends remains deeply rooted in a volunteer\-powered model. While staff coordinate critical material supports, including bond, commissary and phone support, and direct aid to detained immigrants and their families, volunteers are central to advancing the organization’s mission of lessening the isolation and fear created by anti\-immigrant policies.

As immigration policies and community needs continue to evolve, First Friends seeks a strategic, collaborative, and mission\-driven Executive Director to lead the organization through its next chapter of impact and sustainability.

The successful candidate will demonstrate strong emotional intelligence, a collaborative leadership style, and a deep commitment to the dignity and humanity of immigrants. They are equally comfortable representing the organization publicly, partnering with donors and community leaders, and managing the staff, systems, and operations necessary to sustain a growing nonprofit organization.

**Key Responsibilities**

The Executive Director is responsible for the overall leadership, sustainability, and impact of First Friends. This includes setting strategic direction, strengthening the organization's volunteer\-powered model, developing staff, securing resources, stewarding finances, building partnerships, and working closely with the Board of Trustees.

**Volunteer Engagement \& Mobilization**

* Strengthen First Friends' volunteer\-powered model by advancing strategies to engage and retain volunteers.
* Build systems and leadership pathways that expand volunteer impact across programs.
* Foster a culture that recognizes volunteers as essential partners in mission delivery and organizational sustainability.

**Staff Leadership \& Organizational Culture**

* Supervise, coach, and develop a high\-performing staff team.
* Establish clear goals, accountability measures, professional development plans, and regular feedback practices for all staff members.
* Conduct annual and mid\-year performance reviews and foster a collaborative, inclusive, and healthy organizational culture.

**Fundraising, Finance \& Operations**

* Lead fundraising strategy in partnership with the Board and Development Committee, including donor cultivation, grants, and special events.
* In partnership with the Finance Committee, ensure sound financial management, including budgeting, reporting, compliance, and responsible stewardship of organizational resources.
* Manage the organization’s bond program, including tracking bond requests, coordinating and documenting payments, tracking returned bonds.

**Community Partnerships \& Advocacy**

* Build and maintain strong relationships with nonprofit partners, advocacy groups, faith communities, and community stakeholders.
* Represent First Friends in coalitions, public forums, community events, and advocacy efforts.
* Increase visibility and awareness of First Friends' mission, programs, and impact.

**Board Partnership \& Governance**

* Serve as the primary liaison between staff and the Board of Trustees.
* Provide timely information, recommendations, and updates to support effective board decision\-making.
* Partner with the Board on strategic priorities, governance, fundraising, organizational performance, and board development.

**Qualifications**

Qualified candidates will demonstrate a strong commitment to the mission of First Friends and a track record of effective nonprofit leadership.

**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor's degree in a relevant field.
* Three or more years of progressively responsible leadership experience
* Demonstrated success managing staff, volunteers, budgets, operations, and external relationships.
* Proven fundraising experience, including donor cultivation, grants, and institutional funding.
* Strong written, verbal, interpersonal, and public speaking skills.
* Ability to lead strategically while managing day\-to\-day organizational priorities.
* Commitment to immigrant dignity, human rights, and social justice.

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Five or more years experience in nonprofit, community\-based, advocacy, or mission\-driven organizations.
* Experience in immigrant rights, detention support, refugee services, legal services, or related social work.
* Experience leading volunteer\-intensive organizations.
* Familiarity with the immigration landscape in New Jersey, New York, and nationally.
* Experience working with nonprofit boards and governance structures.
* Bilingual proficiency in Spanish
* Candidates with lived experience navigating the immigration system are strongly encouraged to apply

**Working Conditions**

* Day\-to\-day work may be performed remotely, with regular availability for virtual meetings, staff supervision, board engagement, and organizational operations.
* In\-person attendance is required for key activities, including community events, fundraising events, board meetings, volunteer engagement activities, partner meetings, and public representation of First Friends.
* Travel throughout New Jersey and New York is required.
* The organization supports a healthy work\-life balance while recognizing the flexibility required in nonprofit leadership roles.

Pay: $85,000\.00 \- $95,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Paid time off
* Parental leave

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Kearny, NJ 07032

new_jobs.title: Executive Director, Communications and Public Affairs
new_jobs.description:
About Us:

Albert Einstein College of Medicine seeks a strategic, collaborative, and mission\-driven communications leader to serve as Executive Director of the Philip and Rita Rosen Department of Communications and Public Affairs. Reporting to the Sr. Associate Dean, Finance \& Operations, the Executive Director will lead integrated communications to elevate Einstein’s reputation as a premier academic medical and scientific research institution.  

This executive will lead a team of 18 to shape and elevate the reputation of Einstein as an innovative hub for biomedical research, medical and graduate education, and community engagement. The Executive Director will oversee communications \& PR, web strategy \& development, and creative services teams. The department’s work encompasses Einstein’s website and intranet, media relations and social media, printed and digital publications, multimedia production, graphic design and branding, internal and executive communications, and institutional messaging.  

The position requires a seasoned communicator with exceptional judgment, professionalism, and experience navigating complex, matrixed environments involving faculty, researchers, administrative leaders, students, donors, and external stakeholders. The leader will work collaboratively and productively with administrative colleagues and senior faculty, —including deans, department chairs, and center directors, to advance the business goals and strategic priorities of the institution, including philanthropy. The leader will establish and maintain positive and collaborative relationships with their marketing and public relations counterparts at Montefiore Einstein, the umbrella organization for Einstein and Montefiore Health System.  

The Executive Director will assume leadership roles for strategic plan initiatives, join leadership committees associated with academic accreditation, and provide strategic council for major institutional initiatives. The role will also act as the college’s communications lead for crisis and emergency communications.
POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES:
**Strategic \& Executive Communications Leadership**  

Develop and execute a comprehensive, integrated communications and public relations strategy aligned with Einstein’s mission, vision, and strategic priorities.  

Advance institutional reputation and visibility nationally and internationally across biomedical research and medical and graduate education.  

  

Partner closely with senior leadership to shape institutional messaging and thought leadership initiatives.  

  

Develop speeches, presentations, talking points, op\-eds, correspondence, and strategic messaging for senior leadership and the Dean’s Office.  

  

Ensure consistency of messaging across all institutional platforms and stakeholder audiences.  

Financial \& Resource Management  

  

Manage the department’s budget in accordance with college policies and guidelines.  

  

Identify cost\-savings opportunities and ensure responsible stewardship of resources.  

  

Ensure operational effectiveness and continuous improvement. **Media Relations \& Public Affairs**  

  

Lead proactive and reactive media relations efforts involving scientific discoveries, faculty expertise, educational innovation, community engagement, and institutional milestones.  

  

Manage crisis and emergency communications in coordination with Montefiore Einstein’s public relations department and emergency management committee.  

  

Prepare leadership for media interviews, public appearances, and high\-profile engagements.
Content \& Digital Strategy  

  

Oversee institutional storytelling across digital, print, social, video, and multimedia channels.  

  

Collaborate with development and alumni affairs, admissions, faculty affairs, and research leadership to amplify Einstein’s impact and achievements.  

  

Guide editorial strategy highlighting faculty research, student success, community engagement, and scientific innovation. **Team Leadership**  

  

Lead and mentor communications and public relations staff while fostering a culture of creativity, responsiveness, collaboration, and accountability.  

  

Manage freelancers and and vendors as needed.
QUALIFICATIONS:

Bachelor’s degree in communications, journalism, public relations, marketing, or related fields.  

Minimum 15 years of progressively senior communications and/or marketing leadership experience.  

  

Experience in academic medicine, higher education, healthcare, biomedical research, or related mission\-driven organizations.  

  

Demonstrated success leading integrated communications and media strategies in complex organizations.  

  

Exceptional writing, editing, and executive communication skills.  

  

Proven crisis communications and reputation management experience.  

  

Experience advising senior executives and institutional leaders.  

Preferred Qualifications:  

  

Master’s degree preferred.  

  

Experience within an academic medical center, medical school, or research institution is strongly preferred.  

  

Experience supporting fundraising or advancement communications initiatives.

Albert Einstein College of Medicine considers factors such as (but not limited to) scope and responsibilities of the position, candidate's work experience, education/training, key skills, internal peer equity, as well as, market and organizational considerations when extending an offer. Minimum Salary Range: USD $210,000\.00/Yr. Maximum Salary Range: USD $240,000\.00/Yr.

new_jobs.title: Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
Description
  
  

**LOCATION:** Hybrid\* On\-site at least 2 days/week, Washington, DC Metro Area
  
  

About Wendt Center for Loss \& Healing
  
  

Since 1975, the Wendt Center for Loss and Healing, a 501(c)(3\) non\-profit, has helped people in the Washington metropolitan area rebuild a sense of safety and hope after experiencing the death of a loved one, life\-threatening illness, violence, or other trauma.
  
  

We believe that healing from life’s worst moments is possible.
  
We express kindness to all. Caring and compassion are at the heart of all we do.
  
We practice collaboration and teamwork, recognizing that none of us can do it alone.
  
We strive to be lifelong learners and actively seek opportunities to expand our personal and professional knowledge
  
  

About the Position
  
  

The Wendt Center is seeking an experienced, proactive, people\-centered Director of Development to lead its development strategy, cultivate donor engagement, and build strong systems for the effective operations of a dedicated Development team. As a key member of the senior leadership team, the Director of Development will work in partnership with the Executive Director to ensure the Wendt Center’s long\-term financial sustainability and uphold its commitment to community.
  
  

**Important to know:**  

The Wendt Center operates on an October 1 – September 30 fiscal year, with an annual budget of $6M. Our three primary revenue types are: government and foundation grants (accounting for approx. 60 \- 70%); annual Benefit Gala ticket sales and donations (10 \- 15%); and counseling fees (10 – 15%)
  
  

**The Development Team of three includes:** Manager, Institutional Giving, Development Operations Associate, and Volunteer Coordinator (PT). We feel confident in being able to maintain existing funding, with the goal of expanding our individual donor/major donor base and identifying new corporate and foundation partnership opportunities.
  
  

**Primary Responsibilities:**  

Development Strategy, Planning \& Execution
  
  

Serve as a key member of the senior leadership team, partnering with the Executive Director in developing a data\-informed, pipeline\-driven annual budget with revenue goals aligned with mission, organizational growth needs, and staff capacity.
  
In collaboration with the Executive Director and Development team, create and implement a comprehensive development program and fundraising initiatives to proactively engage existing supporters and cultivate new funder prospects (with focus on corporate and partners and individual donors/major gifts) to meet $6 M revenue goal, with year over year growth.
  
Work in close partnership with the Clinical Services team to understand program services and align with fundraising goals, activities, and communications.
  
Work with finance to monitor budget, provide regular reporting on fundraising progress toward goals, and adjust fundraising plans and expectations as needed.
  
Serve as the staff lead for the Development Committee of the Board of Directors, providing development presentations and reports to the Board, identify optimal opportunities to engage and support the Board in targeted fundraising initiatives.
  
Serve as staff lead and visible relationship\-builder for the Center’s annual Benefit/Gala, which provides an important opportunity for our community to come together, and supports general operating expenses. Maintain knowledge of current and emerging fundraising trends and sector best practices.
  
Ensure Wendt’s fundraising efforts and operations work together to demonstrate our mission and clearly tell the story of our impact.
  
  

Development Operations
  
  

Assess donor database for effectiveness; identify opportunities to best leverage existing fundraising software and tools.
  
Create systems, processes, and structures that enable Development team members to effectively track, monitor, and grow funder portfolio and pipeline.
  
Establish protocols and templates to support staff capacity and replicable /scalable fundraising activities.
  
Establish policies and procedures to reinforce equitable and consistent fundraising practices.
  
Ensure maintenance of clean donor data, donation receipts and acknowledgments, and grant management materials for strong record\-keeping and support of the organization’s annual audit.
  
  

Team Management
  
  

Represent the Wendt Center’s values of kindness, collaboration, and practice as a life\-long learner.
  
Build authentic relationships with others in an inclusive environment based on aligned values, mutual trust and respect.
  
Actively lead and engage Development team by providing a clear vision, shared expectations, transparent communication, mentorship/coaching, culture building, and psychological safety.
  
Regularly evaluate staff capacity; identify hiring and professional development needs.
  
  

**Desired Qualifications:**  

A deep commitment to Wendt Center’s mission
  
7\+ years nonprofit development/fundraising experience; demonstrated success in individual/major gifts and corporate donor cultivation a plus
  
3\+ years supervisory experience
  
Strong management and leadership skills, both strategic and operational, with a results orientation and ability to foster inclusive environments across diverse communities, and motivate a team of staff, consultants, and volunteers
  
Excellent relationship building skills, with comfort and interest in engaging with external stakeholders
  
Excellent communications skills with an ability to effectively listen and influence.
  
Proven ability to cultivate and steward charitable contributions and other funding at the $5,000\+ and six\-figure level
  
A commitment to professional ethics in working with confidential, sensitive information
  
Experience managing pipelines and donor management systems; experience with Blackbaud/Raiser's Edge a plus
  
Experience with fundraising event management a plus
  
  

Benefits

Each of our women journeys through a 12\-month residential program of treatment and support services and is fully embraced by a broad community of volunteers; mentors; classroom instructors; trained counselors; and thoughtful programming for school\-age kids to guide these families on a path to real change.

new_jobs.description:
**Deputy Director** **\-** **(****26141848****)**  

Few positions in Montana state government touch more of what makes Montana, Montana. The Deputy Director of The Montana Department of Natural Resources and Conservation helps lead the agency responsible for the state’s land, water, forests, and wildland fire protection — 5\.2 million surface acres of state trust land, water rights administration statewide, and a network of 35 field offices serving communities in every corner of the state. The work carries substantial economic impact and generational consequence: DNRC’s mission is to help ensure Montana’s land and water resources provide benefits for present and future generations.


We are seeking a proven executive to serve as the Department’s second\-in\-command — a leader of leaders who can run day\-to\-day operations of a complex, geographically distributed organization while advising the Director on the issues that shape Montana’s natural resource future.

**The Role:**


The Deputy Director is the principal advisor to the Director and has primary responsibility for the Department’s day\-to\-day operations. The Deputy assists with planning, administration, personnel management, budget development, communications, and establishing goals and direction for the Department; serves as Acting Director in the Director’s absence; and acts on behalf of the Director at internal and external meetings, before the Legislature, and with federal, tribal, and local partners.


A typical day may involve solving complex natural resource management issues with substantial economic impacts; resolving constituent conflicts or complaints; state personnel policy and pay decisions; long\-range program and fiscal strategy; internal and external communication; and management of substantial real estate, physical infrastructure, and agency liability. The Deputy regularly resolves competing demands between divisions, allocates agency resources, interprets and enforces agency policy, and responds to the Governor’s Office, constituent groups, state legislators, Montana’s congressional delegation, and state, federal, and tribal partners.

**What You’ll Walk Into:**
==========================


This is a consequential moment to join DNRC’s executive team. Among the priorities the next Deputy Director will help lead:

* **The upcoming legislative session:** the Deputy coordinates the Department’s biennial budget preparation and legislative proposals with the Governor’s Office and division administrators and represents the Department at hearings and committee meetings.
* **Statewide operations at scale:** the Deputy supports five divisions and 35 field offices spanning trust land management, forestry and fire protection, water resources, conservation, and oil and gas conservation.
* **Coordinating and implementing organizational efficiencies:** the Deputy identifies operational efficiencies, including opportunities in fiscal management to create transparency and sound budget management practices for an annual budget of more than $100 million.

**Who You’ll Lead:**
====================


The Deputy Director reports to the Director and directly supervises the Chief Financial Officer, Communications Director, Safety Manager, Geographic Information System (GIS) Manager, two Administrative Law Judges, and a Legal Secretary, and indirectly supervises the agency's Human Resources Officer, providing oversight of administrative, technical, and professional work performed across the Department.

**What We’re Looking For:**
===========================

* Successful candidates will be able to demonstrate:
* A track record of leading operations across a large, complex, or geographically distributed organization, including supervising senior leaders.
* Experience presenting budgets, legislation, or policy proposals to elected bodies, governing boards, or oversight agencies, as well as navigating the legislative process.
* A history of successfully managing high\-stakes issues to resolution with a high degree of integrity, including issues where stakeholders hold polarized views of what constitutes reasonable use of land and water.
* The ability to build and sustain effective working relationships with staff at every level and with external partners such as elected officials, county commissioners, and tribal leadership.
* Advanced skill in planning, prioritizing, and goal setting, and high proficiency in organizational awareness, conflict management, negotiation, and energizing others.
* Demonstrated excellence in oral and written communication.

 **APPLICATION MATERIALS REQUIRED FOR THIS POSITION ARE:**

* **Cover Letter**\- In your cover letter, please describe your experience leading operations in a complex organization and your experience with legislative or budget processes.
* **Resume**
 **TIP: When attaching your resume, cover letter, and other material, you must mark the attachments as "relevant".**

  

The selection process includes, at minimum, an in\-person panel interview and a second interview with the Director; finalists may be asked to complete a brief work\-sample exercise. **Benefits:**  

Live and work in Helena, Montana, which offers a unique blend of opportunities and natural beauty, situated between Yellowstone and Glacier National Parks. Montana offers abundant recreational opportunities, including camping, fishing, hunting, mountain biking, and hiking.  

Additional benefits include:* Work/life balance.
* Generous paid leave (vacation, sick, holidays), with vacation leave increasing with service.
* Longevity pay with continued state service.
* Health, dental, vision, life insurance, and flexible spending accounts.
* Access to free employee medical clinics and telehealth.
* Retirement plans (Defined Benefit and Defined Contribution plus an optional 457(b) Plan).
* Public Service Loan Forgiveness \- may qualify you for student loan forgiveness.
* Career progression, professional development, and state employe

**About the Role**
------------------



As the **Executive Director**, you will oversee all strategic, financial, and operational aspects of the ProAmpac Employee Assistance Fund (PEAF). You will carry out the vision of the organization, leading efforts to scale the fund globally, engage our workforce, and ensure the fund's long\-term sustainability.



Whether you are navigating compliance, collaborating with the Board of Directors, or visiting our manufacturing sites to raise awareness, your leadership will directly support employees when they need it most.

  


**Essential Duties and Responsibilities**
-----------------------------------------


### **1\. Strategic Leadership \& Global Expansion**


* **Drive Vision:** Provide overall leadership and direction for the PEAF, aligning the program with ProAmpac's core values: Integrity, Intensity, Innovation, Involvement, and Impact.
* **Expand Our Reach:** Lead the expansion of the fund to international locations and newly acquired manufacturing sites, adapting processes to respect regional HR policies and practices.
* **Growth Strategy:** Develop and execute strategic plans to scale program participation, overall funding, and community impact.

### **2\. Program Administration \& Compliance**


* **Daily Operations:** Oversee the day\-to\-day administration of the grant process, ensuring applications are handled fairly, efficiently, and with the **strictest confidentiality**.
* **Regulatory Compliance:** Maintain rigorous records of all grants, donor contributions, and fund activities. Ensure full compliance with nonprofit governance, tax regulations, and legal guidelines.
* **Digital Resources:** Manage the PEAF website and promotional materials to ensure easy, seamless access to applications and contribution portals.

### **3\. Board Governance \& Financial Sustainability**


* **Board Collaboration:** Partner closely with the Board of Directors to guide policy development, long\-term planning, and strategic decisions.
* **Reporting:** Prepare and deliver program metrics, financial updates, and impact reports for board meetings.
* **Fundraising:** Design and champion creative fundraising initiatives, including annual donor campaigns, employee payroll deduction programs, and special events.

### **4\. Employee Engagement \& Advocacy**


* **Raise Awareness:** Partner with HR and plant managers to educate our workforce on how to contribute to the fund and how to apply for support.
* **In\-Person Outreach:** Conduct site visits, deliver presentations, and host employee meetings to build trust and drive engagement across all locations.

  




**What You Bring to the Team**
------------------------------


### **Qualifications \& Experience**


* **Education:** Bachelor’s degree in Nonprofit Management, Business Administration, Human Resources, or a closely related field preferred.
* **Experience:** Proven experience managing charitable programs, foundation grants, or corporate employee assistance funds (**required**).
* **Global Exposure:** Demonstrated ability to implement initiatives or lead programs across global, multi\-site, or newly acquired organizational footprints.
* **Financial Acumen:** Strong program administration skills with experience in budgeting, financial reporting, and donor tracking.

  




### **Key Competencies**


* **Strategic Leadership:** Ability to think long\-term and transition ideas into highly structured, compliant global programs.
* **Empathetic Communication:** Exceptional relationship\-building skills to connect with plant floor employees, executive leadership, and board members alike.
* **Nonprofit Governance:** Deep understanding of regulatory compliance, IRS guidelines for charitable funds, and board administration.

  




**Metrics for Success**
-----------------------


* **Growth** in active employee participation and total recurring contributions.
* **Awareness** increases across all manufacturing and corporate sites.
* **Efficiency** in processing and distributing critical grants equitably.
* **Seamless onboarding** of global sites and newly acquired locations into the program.

  




**Travel \& Physical Requirements**
-----------------------------------


* **Travel:** Ability to travel **20–25%** to various corporate and manufacturing locations to promote fund awareness and build community relationships.

new_jobs.title: Development Director
new_jobs.description:
Geneva Lake Conservancy is seeking a strategic, relationship\-driven Development Director to lead and grow a comprehensive fundraising program at a pivotal moment for land and water conservation in Walworth County and beyond.

Since 1977, GLC has protected the glacial lakes, forests, wetlands, working farmlands, and scenic corridors of Walworth County and its surrounding area. Today, we protect 3,745 acres through conservation easements, land ownership, and partnerships, while engaging the community through public nature preserves and programs such as Conservation@Home and Keep It Blue.

This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced fundraising leader to build lasting donor relationships, strengthen board and community engagement, and help scale annual contributed revenue from approximately $1 million toward $2–3 million over the next five years.

**In this role, you will:**

* Lead a multi\-year fundraising strategy aligned with GLC’s mission, growth goals, and conservation priorities.
* Manage and grow a portfolio of major donors, including cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship of five\- and six\-figure gifts.
* Partner with the Executive Director and Board members on donor strategy, prospect development, and fundraising committee engagement.
* Oversee grants, foundation and corporate partnerships, fundraising events, donor communications, and development operations.
* Supervise and support one full\-time development staff member while helping foster a positive, accountable, mission\-driven team culture.

**We’re looking for someone who brings:**

* 5–7\+ years of progressive nonprofit fundraising experience, with demonstrated success securing major gifts.
* Strong relationship\-building skills and comfort working with donors, board members, volunteers, staff, and community partners.
* Experience developing fundraising plans, managing donor pipelines, writing grants, and using CRM systems to track progress and results.
* Clear written and verbal communication skills, sound judgment, and a collaborative leadership style.

Career Opportunity

The Linden Lodge Foundation seeks a Chief Executive Officer to preserve its extraordinary resident\-centered culture while strengthening the organizational capacity needed for long\-term sustainability. We do this through providing a safe, "home\-like" residence and the development of innovative motivational, educational and vocational prospects.

Our goal is to provide a high quality of life for all residents, ongoing improvement and increased self\-esteem and the ultimate elimination of stigma against those with a mental illness. **About ROCKBlue**

ROCKBlue is an international nonprofit organization committed to strengthening water and sanitation utilities so they can deliver reliable, affordable, and sustainable services to the communities they serve. Founded on the belief that durable improvements in water and sanitation come from strong institutions \- not one‑off projects \- ROCKBlue focuses on leadership, governance, performance, and financial sustainability at the utility level.

ROCKBlue works primarily across Africa, partnering directly with utility boards, senior management teams, development partners, and private‑sector actors. Its approach combines long‑term relationships, practical problem‑solving, and results‑driven accountability to help utilities move from chronic underperformance to stable, investable, and well‑governed institutions.

ROCKBlue delivers its mission through a mix of structured capacity‑building programs, peer‑to‑peer utility partnerships, targeted technical assistance, and performance‑oriented advisory services. The organization emphasizes measurable outcomes, leadership development, and operational discipline to ensure that improvements endure beyond the period of external support.

**ROCKBlue Consultancy** is the organization’s fee‑based advisory and professional services arm. It provides specialized technical, strategic, financial, and operational support to utilities, governments, development finance institutions, donors, and private‑sector clients. Revenues generated through the consultancy contribute to ROCKBlue’s financial sustainability while reinforcing and scaling its mission and impact.

**Co‑Managing Director Position Description**

ROCKBlue is seeking two Co‑Managing Directors (Co‑MDs) for the ROCKBlue Consultancy. The Co‑MDs jointly provide strategic, operational, and commercial leadership for the consultancy division, sharing responsibilities to ensure continuity, resilience, and consistent performance. This dual‑leadership model ensures that if one Co‑MD is unavailable or transitions out of the role, the consultancy retains strong, uninterrupted leadership.

Reporting to the President, the Co‑MDs collectively oversee strategic direction, client engagement, and business growth while ensuring that all consultancy activities align with ROCKBlue’s mission of strengthening water and sanitation utilities in underserved communities.

The Co‑MDs work closely together and in direct collaboration with international partners such as the development financial institutions, aid agencies, engineering firms, and nonprofits in the WASH sector. Together, they lead a team of highly skilled professionals, fostering innovation, operational excellence, and global impact.

As core members of ROCKBlue’s senior leadership, the Co‑MDs contribute jointly to organizational strategy, and business development, working closely with the President and CEO to expand ROCKBlue’s reach. This position offers a unique opportunity for shared leadership, cross‑sector collaboration, and real‑world impact on urban water and sanitation solutions.

**Purpose of the Co‑Managing Director Positions**

Together, the Co‑Managing Directors:

* Serve as the senior management leadership of the ROCKBlue Consultancy, jointly responsible for leading an effective, high‑performing, and financially sustainable consultancy.
* Agree amongst themselves on the allocation of tasks resulting from their position.
* Support the President in defining the consultancy’s strategic vision and are jointly accountable for its implementation and results.
* Set and model the consultancy’s ethical, professional, and operational standards in alignment with ROCKBlue’s long‑term strategic objectives.
* Act as joint spokespersons for the ROCKBlue Consultancy on the policies and objectives agreed with the President.
* Represent the consultancy to clients, partners, and other stakeholders, actively listening to, promoting, and advancing the consultancy’s work.

The Co‑MDs are expected to coordinate closely, clearly allocate responsibilities between themselves, and ensure transparency and continuity in decision‑making.

**Strategic Leadership \& Business Development**With the help of the business development team members, jointly drive business efforts, ensuring revenue growth, client acquisition, and successful execution of contracts while maintaining high service quality.

**Operational \& HR Optimization**Establish and maintain efficient organizational structures, strengthen performance management frameworks, and implement policies to attract, retain, and develop team members.

**Innovation \& Growth Strategy**Lead the development of new funding streams, enhance client confidence through effective contract execution, and implement strategies to drive long‑term sustainability and growth of the consultancy.

--------------

  


FSU Division of University Advancement


Florida State University is currently in the leadership phase of a comprehensive campaign. As the leadership phase of the campaign begins, the Division of University Advancement is accelerating the hiring of additional development officers’ campus\-wide and is excited to build out its team. For Advancement professionals who are interested in being a part of a comprehensive campaign from the beginning for their career development, the timing is ideal to join our team.

The Senior Director of Development strategically designs and executes a personal solicitation program resulting in philanthropic support through the identification, cultivation, solicitation and stewardship of high\-level prospects for the Herbert Wertheim College of Business. The Senior Director of Development secures, on an annual basis, private support at the major gift level ($100,000\) while also identifying, developing and managing a portfolio primarily consisting of $1,000,000 and above donors. Serving as the chief strategist, develops, oversees and coordinates the fundraising plans of the College with the Dean and the Associate Vice President, Colleges, Schools and Units. Prepares prospect materials for the President of the University, the Vice President, the Associate Vice President, the Dean and other senior administration as appropriate to a prospect’s situation.  

Effectively communicates FSU’s mission and represents the FSU’s culture to external stakeholders.

Master's degree and four years of experience in a related advancement field or a Bachelor's degree and six years of related experience. Higher education can substitute for experience at a comparable rate.

**Who We Are**

Outlook Advisory Group (OAG) provides Fractional COO services for law firms and is a trusted consulting partner to organizations across the legal sector. We deliver high\-level advisory services that drive performance, support sustainable growth, and elevate operational integrity. We operate in the space between small, founder\-led operations and law firms who have grown large enough to hire a full executive suite. Our typical clients are small\- to mid\-size law firms who are eager to add efficiency and scale with a commitment to doing things differently. Based in Charlotte, NC and serving clients virtually nationwide, we provide a tailored approach to each engagement, diving deep with law firm Owners/Founders/Managing Partners on strategic planning, business advisory, leadership development, process design and improvement, data and metrics, and discerning implementation of key initiatives. Our clients stay with us for the long\-term, typically 2\+ years, so it’s important to build unparalleled relationships and trust embedded in the client’s organization.

We are committed to taking the burden off the shoulders of law firm owners so they can reclaim their time and return to visionary, strategic work. Operating as true partners within our clients’ firms, we guide our client organizations to be the best they can be, bridging the gap between high\-level strategy, day\-to\-day operations, and complex technology and processes. OAG cuts through backend chaos to establish clear workflows, from software optimization to HR compliance. We are mission\-driven and operate with a high level of integrity and as trusted advisors. We are committed to results and to building a connected culture.

**Who We Want**

We are seeking a Fractional COO Consultant with a strong background in law firm/legal operations at the executive level, with knowledge of law firm\-specific processes, language, and liability prevention. Preferably, the COO Consultant has experience scaling a firm for growth and optimizing their operational playbook. Our ideal candidate is strategic, calm, discerning, motivational, fast\-paced, tech\-forward, and a trusted advisor with comfort interfacing with CEOs and Founders at an executive level.

This is a hands\-on role shaping approximately 4 to 7 law firms at a time via dedicated strategy and implementation, leveraging the firm’s internal resources as well as technology and excellent time management and prioritization. Being a fractional executive provides the opportunity to guide multiple organizations simultaneously. Our team is low\-ego and committed to constant learning and improvement.

**Responsibilities**

* Maintain excellent client communication and develop strong, trusting relationships with clients.

**Qualifications**

* At least a bachelor’s degree in Management, Operations, Accounting, Communications, or a related field; MBA preferred.
* At least 5 years of experience in law firm(s); with at least 2 in an executive leadership role.

* Solution\-focused, and non\-judgmental approach to client work.
* High level of professionalism, including while working remotely, with elevated business style of dress and polished presentation to clients, potential clients, and vendors.
* Willingness to be an additional face of the brand and public ambassador.

**Why Outlook Advisory Group**

At Outlook Advisory Group, you’ll have the opportunity to build something great from the ground up. As a rapidly scaling organization, you will benefit from direct contact and collaboration/mentorship with the Founder and other team members. We are building and evolving our systems as the business grows, and you’ll have the opportunity to provide input and shape how we build. We expect those who invest in this role and our organization will experience a commensurate upside. We support continuous development and certifications, networking and being a connector in the industry, and a flexible schedule to meet client needs.

We are open to different work arrangements, whether standard W\-2 employment or full\-time or part\-time contractor setup, dependent on business and candidates’ mutual workload capacity and scheduling needs.
